# RS-MCA Proximity Prize Research

This is a dedicated Fable/Codex co-director workspace for RS-MCA proximity prize research.

Keep this lane isolated from Erdős 361, Erdős 346, and Random. Treat files in this folder as source/evidence for this workspace only.

content.content:
1	# Director State
2	
3	Project: RS-MCA Proximity Prize Research
4	Status: active co-director loop
5	Updated: 2026-06-19
6	
7	Current target: Cycle 43 reserve-lift homerun against
8	`W-F1-AA-RES-STRUCTURED-COSUPPORT-RESERVE-LIFT`.
9	
10	Cycle 42 external 5.5 Pro round conservatively banks the fixed restricted
11	`t=2,j=4` branch as a local closure:
12	
13	```text
14	q_gen = p,
15	B = F_p,
16	F = F_{p^2},
17	q_line = p^2,
18	D = F_p,
19	t = sigma = 2,
20	j = 4,
21	N_split(p)=p^2/24+O(p^(3/2)).
22	```
23	
24	This cuts the false A-side route `raw G2/G3 failure => Subcase A cannot
25	globalize`. It is not a corrected-reserve, fixed-rate, generated-field,
26	MCA/list/line-decoding/curve-MCA, protocol, SNARK, prize, or final
27	`COUNTERPACKET` theorem.
28	
29	Active run:
30	none at this snapshot. Next prompt:
31	`current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/prompts/20260618_cycle43_reserve_lift_homerun.md`.
32	
33	Working source snapshots:
34	
35	- `upstream_main_20260618/` is `origin/main` after Przemyslaw's manual integration commit `84b6dfa`.
36	- `current_repo_snapshot/` is the local pre-fetch snapshot containing the original PR #62 bundle.
37	- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/` contains the audited Cycle 1 Fable-loop material.
38	
39	Use `upstream_main_20260618/` first. Main papers remain unchanged; new material under `experimental/` is audit/experimental unless independently verified.
40	
41	Primary live route:
42	
43	1. F1 unrestricted same-numerator extension-line lift is COUNTERPACKET/ROUTE_CUT.
44	2. Fixed-rate sigma=1 extension counterexamples are banked as experimental/audit material.
45	3. Unbalanced extension denominators reduce to a residual list object.
46	4. Balanced monic-anchor denominator data reduce to a base-field `hat E = lcm(E,E^tau)` readout at effective prefix `<= 2 sigma`.
47	5. Cycle 1 banked a narrow quadratic reduction: arbitrary anchors factor through a paired base interpolation-residue readout modulo `Ehat=lcm(E,E^tau)`.
48	6. Cycle 5 sharpened the live gap to `W-F1-AA-RES`: prove or refute a reserve-indexed rigidity/value-count law for the paired readout on the bad line.
49	7. Cycle 6B recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after a malformed VS Code terminal artifact. Source audit shows the same-slope kernel `E*F_{<k}[X]` is only a tautological kernel/descent statement.
50	8. Cycle 7 recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after the terminal
51	   `response.md` lost spaces and duplicated repaint fragments. Source audit
52	   rejects the model's claimed `BANKABLE_LEMMA`: the nonconstant CRT multiplier
53	   `theta in B[X]/Ehat` cannot be absorbed into an ordinary pointwise word
54	   `w0+theta*w1`, so `thm:exactcount` does not transfer verbatim. The live
55	   target is now `W-F1-AA-RES-TWISTED-READOUT`.
56	9. Cycle 8 proves that the twist is isomorphic to the original extension
57	   residue count: `B[X]/Ehat ~= F[X]/E` and
58	   `[interp_S(w0)]_Ehat + theta [interp_S(w1)]_Ehat` maps to
59	   `[interp_S(w0)+alpha interp_S(w1)]_E`.
60	10. Cycle 9 proves the locator-quotient decomposition
61	   `W=L_S Q_S+interp_S(w)`, `deg Q_S<=n-a-1`, and cuts raw residue
62	   cardinality as the wrong MCA object. The live target is the bad-line
63	   incidence count
64	   `#{z in F : exists S, [interp_S(w)]_E=z[Bnum]_E}`.
65	11. Cycle 11 closes the first finite line-incidence regime
66	   `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=2`. The quotient has the closed form
67	   `Q_S=C(X-s_T)+C1`, bad-line landing is one conic `det(s_T,p_T)=0`, and
68	   the conditional slope count is `C2=O(n)` with generic `C2<=4`. This is a
69	   BANKABLE_LEMMA / AUDIT only for the low regime, not a proof of `conj:B`.
70	12. Cycle 12 gives the `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=3` quotient/quadric
71	    calculation. `Q_S` depends on `e1(T),e2(T)` and not `e3(T)`, while
72	    bad-line landing is a quadric in `(e1,e2,e3)` with leading coefficient
73	    `wedge([W]_E,[Bnum]_E)`. It does not prove a `C2` bound; the next wall is
74	    slope-fiber collapse on the split-cubic locus.
75	13. Cycle 13 proves the base-component complete-intersection route off
76	    resonance: `Delta=Res(L_T,E)*([I_S]_E wedge [Bnum]_E)`, the resultant is
77	    nonzero on valid co-supports, and after splitting
78	    `Delta=Delta_0+alpha Delta_1`, the two base quadrics are coprime off
79	    `R0 union Ra union Rb`. Therefore `C2=O(p)=O(n)` for `D=F_p`,
80	    `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, away from the classified resonance strata. The
81	    remaining target is the resonance locus where the base quadrics share a
82	    surface component.
83	14. Cycle 14 shows the resonance strata are not excluded by the currently
84	    banked source hypotheses and reduces the residual problem to the explicit
85	    slope equations
86	    `q1 z^2-(p1-q2)z-p2=0`, `tau_3=p1-zq1 in B` on a base surface
87	    `Sigma subset B^3`. The live wall is to prove `O(p)` slope image on
88	    every source-valid resonance surface or construct a source-valid
89	    `Theta(p^2)` slope counterpacket.
90	15. Cycle 15 sharpens that surface slope problem to the affine equation
91	    `L_z(tau)=iota-z mu=0` in `A=F[X]/E`. The `B`-linear columns
92	    `c1(z),c2(z),c3(z)` and determinant consistency polynomial
93	    `Q(z_0,z_1)=det[c1|c2|c3|c0]` are now the live invariant. Rank `3` alone
94	    is not enough: rank `3` with `Q!=0` gives a curve-sized slope set, while
95	    rank `3` with `Q==0` identically is the possible `Theta(q_line)` regime.
96	16. Cycle 16 banks the safe side: off `R0`, if `Q` is not identically zero,
97	    then `C2<=4p=O(p)=O(n)` in the `D=F_p`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3` test regime.
98	    It sharpens the residual wall to the `Q==0` branch restricted to distinct
99	    `D`-split cubics. The proposed trace/Gram criterion for `Q==0` is useful
100	    but remains AUDIT until checked by a local symbolic/finite verifier.
101	
102	17. Cycle 17 attempted a rank-det split scanner but produced no clean
103	    `response.md` and no `output_files/` deliverable. It remains AUDIT only.
104	18. Cycle 18 banks monicity of the `tau_3` determinant in the
105	    `{[W]_E,b}` basis and sharpens the residual branch to resonance
106	    slope-map collapse.
107	19. Cycle 19 proposes rank-one/gate formulas but is AUDIT only.
108	20. Cycle 20 banks the rank-one leading coefficient, quadric-branch normal
109	    form, and the `D` gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D`.
110	21. Cycle 21 banks differential collapse gates: simultaneous branch collapse
111	    is governed by `J_A=J_Aprime=0`, and the resultant of those gates is the
112	    Cycle 20 gate `D`. It does not bank the stronger independence claim.
113	22. Cycle 22 banks the decoupling identity
114	    `Im_alpha(J_A)=2 Im_alpha(d)+Im_alpha(c) f_2` on `Delta1==0`. Therefore,
115	    on the explicit stratum `c in B`, `d notin B`, `Delta1==0`, `D=0`,
116	    alignment is impossible in odd characteristic if that stratum is
117	    source-valid and nonempty. It does not bank nonemptiness or a
118	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
119	23. Cycle 23 proves the Cycle 22 `c in B`, `d notin B` nonemptiness wall is
120	    empty. In that branch `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)` and
121	    `D=-mu^2(c^2/4-d)kappa`, so `D!=0` off `R0`. The only surviving separated
122	    `D=0` lane is now `c notin B`.
123	24. Cycle 24 proves the norm factorization
124	    `D=N(ell)kappa`, with
125	    `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)+delta_c` and
126	    `N(ell)=prod_{a in F_p}E(a)`. Since source-valid denominators are nonzero
127	    on `D=F_p`, `N(ell)!=0`; off `R0`, `kappa!=0`; hence the entire
128	    source-valid `D=0`, off-`R0` branch is empty in this restricted
129	    `t=2,j=3` calculation. The live branch returns to the non-`D=0`
130	    determinant-split `Q==0` wall.
131	25. Cycle 25 banks the six-term Plucker/Laplace expansion of the
132	    slope-fiber determinant `Q(z)` and confirms that the `z`-free determinant
133	    gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D` is separate from `Q(z)`. It also cuts the
134	    false route `Q(z)=0 iff realized slope`: `Q(z)=0` is sufficient only when
135	    the coefficient columns `C(z)=[c_1(z)|c_2(z)|c_3(z)]` have rank `3`; on
136	    rank-drop strata the augmented-rank minors of `[C(z)|c_0(z)]` are required.
137	26. Cycle 26 banks the DEP/NONDEP rank dichotomy. In NONDEP, rank-drop costs
138	    only `O(p)` slopes; away from rank-drop, `Q(z)=0` is necessary and
139	    sufficient for affine `tau in B^3` consistency. The `c notin B`,
140	    `c_b != 0` branch is NONDEP by leading-symbol independence. The proposed
141	    top-degree `Q_4` obstruction is a high-value next target but remains
142	    audit-only until independently verified, and the distinct split-cubic gate
143	    remains separate from affine consistency.
144	27. Cycle 27 identifies a candidate corrected top coefficient
145	    `Q_4=N(c_b)*(Im(d)^2-Im(c)*Im(conj(c)d))`. For `c notin B`, it factors
146	    as `N(c_b)*Im(c)^2*E(-Im(d)/Im(c))`, so `Q_4=0` iff `E` has an `F_p`
147	    root iff the Cycle 24 locator norm `prod_{a in F_p}E(a)` vanishes.
148	    For `c in B`, it becomes `N(c_b)*Im(d)^2`, nonzero under separatedness.
149	    This appears to cut the restricted source-valid `Q==0` branch, but it is
150	    banked only as `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT` until Cycle 28
151	    independently rederives the column algebra.
152	28. Cycle 28 independently confirms the `q1/q2` closed forms from the Cycle 20
153	    wedge identities and verifies the `Q_4` formula. The restricted
154	    `D=F_p`, `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, off-`R0`,
155	    `c_b!=0`, source-valid separated branch is now closed as a local
156	    `PROOF / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT`: `Q` is never identically zero, so Cycle 16
157	    gives `C2<=4p=O(p)`. This is not a corrected-reserve, MCA, protocol, or
158	    prize theorem.
159	29. Cycle 29 shows that the Cycle 28 determinant mechanism changes meaning at
160	    `t=2,j=4`. The quotient depends on `tau_1,tau_2,tau_3`, while the
161	    locator residue introduces `tau_4`, giving a square affine system
162	    `M(z)tau=-C_0(z)` in the four-dimensional `B`-space `A=F[X]/E`. The top
163	    symbol persists as `TopSym(det_B M(z))=-N(kappa)N(z)^2Q_4`, with the
164	    same source-valid nonzero Cycle 28 locator quantity, but this only proves
165	    generic invertibility. It does not imply an `O(p)` slope bound for `j=4`.
166	    The live wall is the split-quartic gate for the rational preimage
167	    `tau(z)`.
168	30. Cycle 30 reduces the `t=2,j=4` split-quartic gate to the explicit
169	    one-`F`-quadric / two-`B`-quadric equation
170	    `Phi(tau)=iota wedge_F mu=0`, equivalently
171	    `kappa*N(lambda)-(ell[Q_S]_E) wedge_F (b lambda)=0`, with
172	    `lambda=[L_T]_E`. This banks the gate reduction only. Codex direct
173	    finite scans for random small source-valid instances lean toward
174	    `O(p)`-scale realized slope counts (`p=29` average about `1.08p`, not a
175	    positive-density fraction of `p^2`). The live wall is now to explain this
176	    split-locus collapse or construct a genuine source-valid `Theta(q_line)`
177	    growing-prime family.
178	31. Cycle 31 challenges the Cycle 30 scan interpretation and proposes the
179	    quartic monodromy invariant as the right object. Codex audit does not bank
180	    the claim that `O(p)` collapse is impossible and does not bank a
181	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. It does bank the next exact wall:
182	    compute/source-audit `disc_X L_{tau(z)}`, the resolvent cubic, the correct
183	    base-field model for `z in F_{p^2}` with splitting over `B=F_p`, and the
184	    geometric monodromy group.
185	32. Cycle 32 cuts the one-variable `B(z)` / `F(z)` framing. The correct model
186	    is a two-dimensional `B`-surface over `A^2_B`, with
187	    `z=z_0+alpha z_1`. Codex added a local Cramer-system factorization
188	    histogram checker. The checker matches direct support enumeration away
189	    from the determinant curve `Delta=0`; discrepancies are recorded as
190	    `singular_split_C2`. This is `ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL /
191	    AUDIT`, not proof of `S_4`, not proof of positive density, and not a
192	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
193	33. Cycle 33 banks the singular determinant curve bound. Under the Cycle 29
194	    top-symbol nonzero hypotheses, `Delta(z_0,z_1)=det_B M(z)` has degree at
195	    most four and is not identically zero, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
196	    `4p=O(p)` split slopes. This is a restricted `BANKABLE_LEMMA /
197	    CONDITIONAL / AUDIT`, not a proof of off-curve positive density or a
198	    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
199	34. Cycle 34 banks the off-curve dominance lemma. On the source-valid dense
200	    open of the restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` branch, the rational map
201	    `psi:z |-> tau(z)=M(z)^(-1)(-C_0(z))` has generic `B`-Jacobian rank two
202	    and is birational onto the Cycle 30 quadric image. This cuts the hidden
203	    rank-one / curve-collapse route for `O(p)` off-curve counts. It does not
204	    prove geometric `S_4`, positive split density, arithmetic/geometric
205	    monodromy equality, or a `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. The live wall is
206	    the geometric monodromy and constant-field test.
207	35. Cycle 35 banks a finite-place S4 certificate. For any fixed source-valid
208	    restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` instance, off-`Delta` squarefree
209	    factorization types `"4"` and `"13"` certify `G_arith=S_4`, and the even
210	    type `"13"` cuts the sign constant-field obstruction, giving
211	    `G_geom=G_arith=S_4` for that instance. This turns the Cycle 32 finite
212	    histogram into serious `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence, but it is not a
213	    uniform-in-`p` theorem and not a `COUNTERPACKET`. The live wall is now to
214	    construct an explicit source-valid growing-prime family with uniform S4,
215	    or identify the exact obstruction.
216	36. Cycle 36 banks an explicit source-valid growing-prime denominator family
217	    for primes `p = 3 mod 4`: `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}=B(alpha)`,
218	    `alpha^2=-1`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`. It reduces uniform geometric S4
219	    to a single-prime finite certificate after good-reduction monotonicity.
220	    Codex corrected the raw shortcut: quartic transitivity/geometric
221	    irreducibility must be certified in addition to resolvent irreducibility
222	    and discriminant nonsquareness. This is not a COUNTERPACKET and not an
223	    MCA/protocol/prize theorem.
224	37. Cycle 37 re-checks the source-valid hand gates for the Cycle 36 explicit
225	    family: `E(a)=(a^2+1)+alpha*a` has no `F_p` root, `E` and `E^tau` are
226	    separated on `D=F_p`, `c=alpha` gives the `c notin B`, `c_b!=0` branch,
227	    and choosing `u=1+X` gives `kappa=1`. With `c=alpha`, `d=1`, the top
228	    symbol quantity remains `Q4=N(c_b)!=0`, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
229	    `4p` slopes in this restricted branch. Cycle 37 did not produce a working
230	    single-prime S4 certificate: the supplied Python checker is type-malformed
231	    and fails by mixing `F`-elements with residue pairs. Status:
232	    `BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT`, not `PROOF`
233	    and not `COUNTERPACKET`.
234	38. Cycle 38 identifies and repairs the exact Cycle 37 checker type error.
235	    The repaired free data are actual `F`-elements
236	    `W_{n-1..n-4}=1,alpha,1+alpha,1`, not residue-pairs. Codex ran the
237	    patched checker locally at `p=31`; it returns
238	    `PASS_S4_finite_place=true` with factorization witnesses `"4"` and
239	    `"13"`, a nonsquare-discriminant witness, and no singular points on the
240	    tested line. This is finite-place `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence plus a
241	    bankable checker repair. It is not a uniform theorem, not geometric `S_4`
242	    over `B(z_0,z_1)`, not a `COUNTERPACKET`, and not an MCA/protocol/prize
243	    theorem.
244	39. Cycle 39 proves the locator-collapse lemma in the explicit `t=2,j=4`
245	    family: `[X^p-X]_E=alpha` when `(-5/p)=+1`, and
246	    `[X^p-X]_E=-2X` when `(-5/p)=-1`. Thus the family splits into two fixed
247	    congruence subcases instead of varying freely with `p`. The `p=31`
248	    certificate covers only Subcase B; Subcase A and good reduction remain
249	    open.
250	
251	Active target:
252	
253	- Cycle 41 homerun characteristic-zero Delta / good-reduction bridge:
254	  `W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-CHAR0DELTA`. The target is to bridge the
255	  finite-place geometric `S_4` certificates from Cycle 40 to characteristic
256	  zero and a growing-prime route, or identify the exact algebraic obstruction.
257	- Secondary target: `W-F1-AA-RES-T3J2`.
258	
259	Active run:

# Director State

Project: RS-MCA Proximity Prize Research
Status: active co-director loop
Updated: 2026-06-19

Current target: Cycle 43 reserve-lift homerun against
`W-F1-AA-RES-STRUCTURED-COSUPPORT-RESERVE-LIFT`.

Cycle 42 external 5.5 Pro round conservatively banks the fixed restricted
`t=2,j=4` branch as a local closure:

```text
q_gen = p,
B = F_p,
F = F_{p^2},
q_line = p^2,
D = F_p,
t = sigma = 2,
j = 4,
N_split(p)=p^2/24+O(p^(3/2)).
```

This cuts the false A-side route `raw G2/G3 failure => Subcase A cannot
globalize`. It is not a corrected-reserve, fixed-rate, generated-field,
MCA/list/line-decoding/curve-MCA, protocol, SNARK, prize, or final
`COUNTERPACKET` theorem.

Active run:
none at this snapshot. Next prompt:
`current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/prompts/20260618_cycle43_reserve_lift_homerun.md`.

Working source snapshots:

- `upstream_main_20260618/` is `origin/main` after Przemyslaw's manual integration commit `84b6dfa`.
- `current_repo_snapshot/` is the local pre-fetch snapshot containing the original PR #62 bundle.
- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/` contains the audited Cycle 1 Fable-loop material.

Use `upstream_main_20260618/` first. Main papers remain unchanged; new material under `experimental/` is audit/experimental unless independently verified.

Primary live route:

1. F1 unrestricted same-numerator extension-line lift is COUNTERPACKET/ROUTE_CUT.
2. Fixed-rate sigma=1 extension counterexamples are banked as experimental/audit material.
3. Unbalanced extension denominators reduce to a residual list object.
4. Balanced monic-anchor denominator data reduce to a base-field `hat E = lcm(E,E^tau)` readout at effective prefix `<= 2 sigma`.
5. Cycle 1 banked a narrow quadratic reduction: arbitrary anchors factor through a paired base interpolation-residue readout modulo `Ehat=lcm(E,E^tau)`.
6. Cycle 5 sharpened the live gap to `W-F1-AA-RES`: prove or refute a reserve-indexed rigidity/value-count law for the paired readout on the bad line.
7. Cycle 6B recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after a malformed VS Code terminal artifact. Source audit shows the same-slope kernel `E*F_{<k}[X]` is only a tautological kernel/descent statement.
8. Cycle 7 recovered a clean structured Claude transcript after the terminal
   `response.md` lost spaces and duplicated repaint fragments. Source audit
   rejects the model's claimed `BANKABLE_LEMMA`: the nonconstant CRT multiplier
   `theta in B[X]/Ehat` cannot be absorbed into an ordinary pointwise word
   `w0+theta*w1`, so `thm:exactcount` does not transfer verbatim. The live
   target is now `W-F1-AA-RES-TWISTED-READOUT`.
9. Cycle 8 proves that the twist is isomorphic to the original extension
   residue count: `B[X]/Ehat ~= F[X]/E` and
   `[interp_S(w0)]_Ehat + theta [interp_S(w1)]_Ehat` maps to
   `[interp_S(w0)+alpha interp_S(w1)]_E`.
10. Cycle 9 proves the locator-quotient decomposition
   `W=L_S Q_S+interp_S(w)`, `deg Q_S<=n-a-1`, and cuts raw residue
   cardinality as the wrong MCA object. The live target is the bad-line
   incidence count
   `#{z in F : exists S, [interp_S(w)]_E=z[Bnum]_E}`.
11. Cycle 11 closes the first finite line-incidence regime
   `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=2`. The quotient has the closed form
   `Q_S=C(X-s_T)+C1`, bad-line landing is one conic `det(s_T,p_T)=0`, and
   the conditional slope count is `C2=O(n)` with generic `C2<=4`. This is a
   BANKABLE_LEMMA / AUDIT only for the low regime, not a proof of `conj:B`.
12. Cycle 12 gives the `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=r-t=3` quotient/quadric
    calculation. `Q_S` depends on `e1(T),e2(T)` and not `e3(T)`, while
    bad-line landing is a quadric in `(e1,e2,e3)` with leading coefficient
    `wedge([W]_E,[Bnum]_E)`. It does not prove a `C2` bound; the next wall is
    slope-fiber collapse on the split-cubic locus.
13. Cycle 13 proves the base-component complete-intersection route off
    resonance: `Delta=Res(L_T,E)*([I_S]_E wedge [Bnum]_E)`, the resultant is
    nonzero on valid co-supports, and after splitting
    `Delta=Delta_0+alpha Delta_1`, the two base quadrics are coprime off
    `R0 union Ra union Rb`. Therefore `C2=O(p)=O(n)` for `D=F_p`,
    `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, away from the classified resonance strata. The
    remaining target is the resonance locus where the base quadrics share a
    surface component.
14. Cycle 14 shows the resonance strata are not excluded by the currently
    banked source hypotheses and reduces the residual problem to the explicit
    slope equations
    `q1 z^2-(p1-q2)z-p2=0`, `tau_3=p1-zq1 in B` on a base surface
    `Sigma subset B^3`. The live wall is to prove `O(p)` slope image on
    every source-valid resonance surface or construct a source-valid
    `Theta(p^2)` slope counterpacket.
15. Cycle 15 sharpens that surface slope problem to the affine equation
    `L_z(tau)=iota-z mu=0` in `A=F[X]/E`. The `B`-linear columns
    `c1(z),c2(z),c3(z)` and determinant consistency polynomial
    `Q(z_0,z_1)=det[c1|c2|c3|c0]` are now the live invariant. Rank `3` alone
    is not enough: rank `3` with `Q!=0` gives a curve-sized slope set, while
    rank `3` with `Q==0` identically is the possible `Theta(q_line)` regime.
16. Cycle 16 banks the safe side: off `R0`, if `Q` is not identically zero,
    then `C2<=4p=O(p)=O(n)` in the `D=F_p`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3` test regime.
    It sharpens the residual wall to the `Q==0` branch restricted to distinct
    `D`-split cubics. The proposed trace/Gram criterion for `Q==0` is useful
    but remains AUDIT until checked by a local symbolic/finite verifier.

17. Cycle 17 attempted a rank-det split scanner but produced no clean
    `response.md` and no `output_files/` deliverable. It remains AUDIT only.
18. Cycle 18 banks monicity of the `tau_3` determinant in the
    `{[W]_E,b}` basis and sharpens the residual branch to resonance
    slope-map collapse.
19. Cycle 19 proposes rank-one/gate formulas but is AUDIT only.
20. Cycle 20 banks the rank-one leading coefficient, quadric-branch normal
    form, and the `D` gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D`.
21. Cycle 21 banks differential collapse gates: simultaneous branch collapse
    is governed by `J_A=J_Aprime=0`, and the resultant of those gates is the
    Cycle 20 gate `D`. It does not bank the stronger independence claim.
22. Cycle 22 banks the decoupling identity
    `Im_alpha(J_A)=2 Im_alpha(d)+Im_alpha(c) f_2` on `Delta1==0`. Therefore,
    on the explicit stratum `c in B`, `d notin B`, `Delta1==0`, `D=0`,
    alignment is impossible in odd characteristic if that stratum is
    source-valid and nonempty. It does not bank nonemptiness or a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
23. Cycle 23 proves the Cycle 22 `c in B`, `d notin B` nonemptiness wall is
    empty. In that branch `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)` and
    `D=-mu^2(c^2/4-d)kappa`, so `D!=0` off `R0`. The only surviving separated
    `D=0` lane is now `c notin B`.
24. Cycle 24 proves the norm factorization
    `D=N(ell)kappa`, with
    `ell=[X^p-X]_E=mu*(xi+c/2)+delta_c` and
    `N(ell)=prod_{a in F_p}E(a)`. Since source-valid denominators are nonzero
    on `D=F_p`, `N(ell)!=0`; off `R0`, `kappa!=0`; hence the entire
    source-valid `D=0`, off-`R0` branch is empty in this restricted
    `t=2,j=3` calculation. The live branch returns to the non-`D=0`
    determinant-split `Q==0` wall.
25. Cycle 25 banks the six-term Plucker/Laplace expansion of the
    slope-fiber determinant `Q(z)` and confirms that the `z`-free determinant
    gate `det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D` is separate from `Q(z)`. It also cuts the
    false route `Q(z)=0 iff realized slope`: `Q(z)=0` is sufficient only when
    the coefficient columns `C(z)=[c_1(z)|c_2(z)|c_3(z)]` have rank `3`; on
    rank-drop strata the augmented-rank minors of `[C(z)|c_0(z)]` are required.
26. Cycle 26 banks the DEP/NONDEP rank dichotomy. In NONDEP, rank-drop costs
    only `O(p)` slopes; away from rank-drop, `Q(z)=0` is necessary and
    sufficient for affine `tau in B^3` consistency. The `c notin B`,
    `c_b != 0` branch is NONDEP by leading-symbol independence. The proposed
    top-degree `Q_4` obstruction is a high-value next target but remains
    audit-only until independently verified, and the distinct split-cubic gate
    remains separate from affine consistency.
27. Cycle 27 identifies a candidate corrected top coefficient
    `Q_4=N(c_b)*(Im(d)^2-Im(c)*Im(conj(c)d))`. For `c notin B`, it factors
    as `N(c_b)*Im(c)^2*E(-Im(d)/Im(c))`, so `Q_4=0` iff `E` has an `F_p`
    root iff the Cycle 24 locator norm `prod_{a in F_p}E(a)` vanishes.
    For `c in B`, it becomes `N(c_b)*Im(d)^2`, nonzero under separatedness.
    This appears to cut the restricted source-valid `Q==0` branch, but it is
    banked only as `BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT` until Cycle 28
    independently rederives the column algebra.
28. Cycle 28 independently confirms the `q1/q2` closed forms from the Cycle 20
    wedge identities and verifies the `Q_4` formula. The restricted
    `D=F_p`, `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, off-`R0`,
    `c_b!=0`, source-valid separated branch is now closed as a local
    `PROOF / ROUTE_CUT / AUDIT`: `Q` is never identically zero, so Cycle 16
    gives `C2<=4p=O(p)`. This is not a corrected-reserve, MCA, protocol, or
    prize theorem.
29. Cycle 29 shows that the Cycle 28 determinant mechanism changes meaning at
    `t=2,j=4`. The quotient depends on `tau_1,tau_2,tau_3`, while the
    locator residue introduces `tau_4`, giving a square affine system
    `M(z)tau=-C_0(z)` in the four-dimensional `B`-space `A=F[X]/E`. The top
    symbol persists as `TopSym(det_B M(z))=-N(kappa)N(z)^2Q_4`, with the
    same source-valid nonzero Cycle 28 locator quantity, but this only proves
    generic invertibility. It does not imply an `O(p)` slope bound for `j=4`.
    The live wall is the split-quartic gate for the rational preimage
    `tau(z)`.
30. Cycle 30 reduces the `t=2,j=4` split-quartic gate to the explicit
    one-`F`-quadric / two-`B`-quadric equation
    `Phi(tau)=iota wedge_F mu=0`, equivalently
    `kappa*N(lambda)-(ell[Q_S]_E) wedge_F (b lambda)=0`, with
    `lambda=[L_T]_E`. This banks the gate reduction only. Codex direct
    finite scans for random small source-valid instances lean toward
    `O(p)`-scale realized slope counts (`p=29` average about `1.08p`, not a
    positive-density fraction of `p^2`). The live wall is now to explain this
    split-locus collapse or construct a genuine source-valid `Theta(q_line)`
    growing-prime family.
31. Cycle 31 challenges the Cycle 30 scan interpretation and proposes the
    quartic monodromy invariant as the right object. Codex audit does not bank
    the claim that `O(p)` collapse is impossible and does not bank a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. It does bank the next exact wall:
    compute/source-audit `disc_X L_{tau(z)}`, the resolvent cubic, the correct
    base-field model for `z in F_{p^2}` with splitting over `B=F_p`, and the
    geometric monodromy group.
32. Cycle 32 cuts the one-variable `B(z)` / `F(z)` framing. The correct model
    is a two-dimensional `B`-surface over `A^2_B`, with
    `z=z_0+alpha z_1`. Codex added a local Cramer-system factorization
    histogram checker. The checker matches direct support enumeration away
    from the determinant curve `Delta=0`; discrepancies are recorded as
    `singular_split_C2`. This is `ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL /
    AUDIT`, not proof of `S_4`, not proof of positive density, and not a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
33. Cycle 33 banks the singular determinant curve bound. Under the Cycle 29
    top-symbol nonzero hypotheses, `Delta(z_0,z_1)=det_B M(z)` has degree at
    most four and is not identically zero, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
    `4p=O(p)` split slopes. This is a restricted `BANKABLE_LEMMA /
    CONDITIONAL / AUDIT`, not a proof of off-curve positive density or a
    `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket.
34. Cycle 34 banks the off-curve dominance lemma. On the source-valid dense
    open of the restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` branch, the rational map
    `psi:z |-> tau(z)=M(z)^(-1)(-C_0(z))` has generic `B`-Jacobian rank two
    and is birational onto the Cycle 30 quadric image. This cuts the hidden
    rank-one / curve-collapse route for `O(p)` off-curve counts. It does not
    prove geometric `S_4`, positive split density, arithmetic/geometric
    monodromy equality, or a `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket. The live wall is
    the geometric monodromy and constant-field test.
35. Cycle 35 banks a finite-place S4 certificate. For any fixed source-valid
    restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` instance, off-`Delta` squarefree
    factorization types `"4"` and `"13"` certify `G_arith=S_4`, and the even
    type `"13"` cuts the sign constant-field obstruction, giving
    `G_geom=G_arith=S_4` for that instance. This turns the Cycle 32 finite
    histogram into serious `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence, but it is not a
    uniform-in-`p` theorem and not a `COUNTERPACKET`. The live wall is now to
    construct an explicit source-valid growing-prime family with uniform S4,
    or identify the exact obstruction.
36. Cycle 36 banks an explicit source-valid growing-prime denominator family
    for primes `p = 3 mod 4`: `B=F_p`, `F=F_{p^2}=B(alpha)`,
    `alpha^2=-1`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`. It reduces uniform geometric S4
    to a single-prime finite certificate after good-reduction monotonicity.
    Codex corrected the raw shortcut: quartic transitivity/geometric
    irreducibility must be certified in addition to resolvent irreducibility
    and discriminant nonsquareness. This is not a COUNTERPACKET and not an
    MCA/protocol/prize theorem.
37. Cycle 37 re-checks the source-valid hand gates for the Cycle 36 explicit
    family: `E(a)=(a^2+1)+alpha*a` has no `F_p` root, `E` and `E^tau` are
    separated on `D=F_p`, `c=alpha` gives the `c notin B`, `c_b!=0` branch,
    and choosing `u=1+X` gives `kappa=1`. With `c=alpha`, `d=1`, the top
    symbol quantity remains `Q4=N(c_b)!=0`, so `Delta=0` contributes at most
    `4p` slopes in this restricted branch. Cycle 37 did not produce a working
    single-prime S4 certificate: the supplied Python checker is type-malformed
    and fails by mixing `F`-elements with residue pairs. Status:
    `BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT`, not `PROOF`
    and not `COUNTERPACKET`.
38. Cycle 38 identifies and repairs the exact Cycle 37 checker type error.
    The repaired free data are actual `F`-elements
    `W_{n-1..n-4}=1,alpha,1+alpha,1`, not residue-pairs. Codex ran the
    patched checker locally at `p=31`; it returns
    `PASS_S4_finite_place=true` with factorization witnesses `"4"` and
    `"13"`, a nonsquare-discriminant witness, and no singular points on the
    tested line. This is finite-place `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence plus a
    bankable checker repair. It is not a uniform theorem, not geometric `S_4`
    over `B(z_0,z_1)`, not a `COUNTERPACKET`, and not an MCA/protocol/prize
    theorem.
39. Cycle 39 proves the locator-collapse lemma in the explicit `t=2,j=4`
    family: `[X^p-X]_E=alpha` when `(-5/p)=+1`, and
    `[X^p-X]_E=-2X` when `(-5/p)=-1`. Thus the family splits into two fixed
    congruence subcases instead of varying freely with `p`. The `p=31`
    certificate covers only Subcase B; Subcase A and good reduction remain
    open.

Active target:

- Cycle 41 homerun characteristic-zero Delta / good-reduction bridge:
  `W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-CHAR0DELTA`. The target is to bridge the

1	# Cycle 40 Subcase Good-Reduction Audit
2	
3	Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT.
4	
5	Harness status: clean enough `artifact_stream`. The run completed with
6	`OK_WITH_NONFATAL_STREAM_WARNING` caused by one malformed stream-json line, but
7	`response.md` was written and contains the theorem answer. There is no
8	terminal/ad transcript involved.
9	
10	Source artifacts:
11	
12	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RESPONSE.md`
13	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RAW.json`
14	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RAW.jsonl`
15	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RUN_RESULT.json`
16	- `local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_from_response.py`
17	- `local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_result.json`
18	
19	## Ledger
20	
21	- `q_gen = p`.
22	- `B = F_p`.
23	- `F = F_{p^2} = B(alpha)`, with `alpha^2 = -1`.
24	- `q_line = |F| = p^2`.
25	- `q_chal`: unused.
26	- Domain: `D = F_p`.
27	- Restricted regime: `t = sigma = 2`, `j = 4`.
28	- Candidate family: primes `p = 3 mod 4`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`,
29	  `u=[W]_E=1+X`, and top free data
30	  `W_{n-1..n-4}=1, alpha, 1+alpha, 1`.
31	
32	This remains a restricted residue-line / surface-monodromy branch. It is not
33	a corrected-reserve theorem, MCA claim, list-decoding claim, CA claim,
34	line-decoding claim, curve-MCA claim, protocol claim, SNARK claim, prize
35	solution, or `COUNTERPACKET`.
36	
37	## Banked Lemma: Finite-Place Geometric S4 Criterion
38	
39	Cycle 40 correctly sharpens the interpretation of the finite-place
40	factorization histograms.
41	
42	Let `p >= 7`, `p` not dividing `24`, and let `L_tau(X)` be the degree-four
43	fiber polynomial along a fixed line in the tested surface. Suppose that, away
44	from nonsquarefree fibers, the factorization histogram over `F_p` contains
45	both:
46	
47	```text
48	"4"    irreducible quartic
49	"13"   linear times irreducible cubic
50	```
51	
52	Then the tested finite-place cover has:
53	
54	```text
55	G_arith = G_geom = S_4.
56	```
57	
58	Reason:
59	
60	1. Type `"4"` gives a 4-cycle, so the arithmetic group is transitive and
61	   contains an odd permutation.
62	2. Type `"13"` gives a 3-cycle.
63	3. A transitive subgroup of `S_4` containing a 3-cycle and a 4-cycle is `S_4`.
64	4. Type `"4"` and type `"13"` have opposite permutation parity. For
65	   squarefree specializations this means the quartic discriminant takes both
66	   nonsquare and square values on the tested line. Hence the squarefree part of
67	   the discriminant is not constant, so the geometric group is not contained
68	   in `A_4`.
69	5. Since `G_geom` is normal in `G_arith=S_4`, the only normal subgroup not
70	   contained in `A_4` is `S_4` itself.
71	
72	This upgrades the already-audited Cycle 38 `p=31` Subcase B certificate from
73	arithmetic `S_4` evidence to finite-place geometric `S_4` evidence.
74	
75	## Codex Local Checker Execution
76	
77	Cycle 40 supplied a parametrized pure-Python checker. Codex extracted it to
78	
79	```text
80	local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_from_response.py
81	```
82	
83	and ran it locally. The result is saved at
84	
85	```text
86	local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_result.json
87	```
88	
89	The checker reports `all_pass=true`.
90	
91	Subcase A (`p=3 mod4`, `p=2,3 mod5`, `ell=alpha`) passes at:
92	
93	```text
94	p=7:  hist {"4":1, "13":2, "112":1}
95	p=23: hist {"4":3, "13":10, "112":7, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":1}
96	p=43: hist {"4":10, "13":16, "22":4, "112":9, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":2}
97	p=47: hist {"4":9, "13":23, "22":6, "112":3, "1111":3}
98	```
99	
100	Subcase B (`p=3 mod4`, `p=1,4 mod5`, `ell=-2X`) passes at:
101	
102	```text
103	p=11: hist {"4":3, "13":5, "22":1, "112":1, "nonsquarefree":1}
104	p=19: hist {"4":4, "13":4, "22":5, "112":3, "1111":1}
105	p=31: hist {"4":6, "13":11, "22":6, "112":5, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":2}
106	p=59: hist {"4":17, "13":19, "22":6, "112":14, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":1}
107	```
108	
109	Therefore Subcase A is no longer merely a proposed scan: it has finite-place
110	geometric `S_4` evidence at multiple primes, including `p=7`.
111	
112	## What Is Not Banked
113	
114	Do not promote Cycle 40 to any global theorem. The following remain unproved:
115	
116	- the characteristic-zero determinant `Delta(z_0,z_1)` for either fixed
117	  subcase cover;
118	- the numerator of `disc_X L_tau` over `Q(i)(z_0,z_1)`;
119	- a good-reduction prime certified from the characteristic-zero branch data;
120	- the tame-specialization bridge from a finite-place `S_4` certificate to the
121	  characteristic-zero geometric group;
122	- the Chebotarev/Lang-Weil density step for a growing-prime set;
123	- any `COUNTERPACKET`, corrected-reserve theorem, MCA/CA/list/line/curve-MCA
124	  claim, protocol claim, SNARK claim, or prize-level solution.
125	
126	## Exact New Wall
127	
128	The live wall is sharpened to:
129	
130	```text
131	W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-CHAR0DELTA
132	```
133	
134	The next theorem-sized target is to compute or certify, separately for
135	`ell=alpha` and `ell=-2X`, the characteristic-zero branch determinant and
136	quartic discriminant data needed to prove good reduction at at least one of
137	the finite primes already carrying geometric `S_4` evidence.

# Cycle 40 Subcase Good-Reduction Audit

Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT.

Harness status: clean enough `artifact_stream`. The run completed with
`OK_WITH_NONFATAL_STREAM_WARNING` caused by one malformed stream-json line, but
`response.md` was written and contains the theorem answer. There is no
terminal/ad transcript involved.

Source artifacts:

- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RESPONSE.md`
- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RAW.json`
- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RAW.jsonl`
- `raw/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_RUN_RESULT.json`
- `local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_from_response.py`
- `local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_result.json`

## Ledger

- `q_gen = p`.
- `B = F_p`.
- `F = F_{p^2} = B(alpha)`, with `alpha^2 = -1`.
- `q_line = |F| = p^2`.
- `q_chal`: unused.
- Domain: `D = F_p`.
- Restricted regime: `t = sigma = 2`, `j = 4`.
- Candidate family: primes `p = 3 mod 4`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`,
  `u=[W]_E=1+X`, and top free data
  `W_{n-1..n-4}=1, alpha, 1+alpha, 1`.

This remains a restricted residue-line / surface-monodromy branch. It is not
a corrected-reserve theorem, MCA claim, list-decoding claim, CA claim,
line-decoding claim, curve-MCA claim, protocol claim, SNARK claim, prize
solution, or `COUNTERPACKET`.

## Banked Lemma: Finite-Place Geometric S4 Criterion

Cycle 40 correctly sharpens the interpretation of the finite-place
factorization histograms.

Let `p >= 7`, `p` not dividing `24`, and let `L_tau(X)` be the degree-four
fiber polynomial along a fixed line in the tested surface. Suppose that, away
from nonsquarefree fibers, the factorization histogram over `F_p` contains
both:

```text
"4"    irreducible quartic
"13"   linear times irreducible cubic
```

Then the tested finite-place cover has:

```text
G_arith = G_geom = S_4.
```

Reason:

1. Type `"4"` gives a 4-cycle, so the arithmetic group is transitive and
   contains an odd permutation.
2. Type `"13"` gives a 3-cycle.
3. A transitive subgroup of `S_4` containing a 3-cycle and a 4-cycle is `S_4`.
4. Type `"4"` and type `"13"` have opposite permutation parity. For
   squarefree specializations this means the quartic discriminant takes both
   nonsquare and square values on the tested line. Hence the squarefree part of
   the discriminant is not constant, so the geometric group is not contained
   in `A_4`.
5. Since `G_geom` is normal in `G_arith=S_4`, the only normal subgroup not
   contained in `A_4` is `S_4` itself.

This upgrades the already-audited Cycle 38 `p=31` Subcase B certificate from
arithmetic `S_4` evidence to finite-place geometric `S_4` evidence.

## Codex Local Checker Execution

Cycle 40 supplied a parametrized pure-Python checker. Codex extracted it to

```text
local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_from_response.py
```

and ran it locally. The result is saved at

```text
local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_result.json
```

The checker reports `all_pass=true`.

Subcase A (`p=3 mod4`, `p=2,3 mod5`, `ell=alpha`) passes at:

```text
p=7:  hist {"4":1, "13":2, "112":1}
p=23: hist {"4":3, "13":10, "112":7, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":1}
p=43: hist {"4":10, "13":16, "22":4, "112":9, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":2}
p=47: hist {"4":9, "13":23, "22":6, "112":3, "1111":3}
```

Subcase B (`p=3 mod4`, `p=1,4 mod5`, `ell=-2X`) passes at:

```text
p=11: hist {"4":3, "13":5, "22":1, "112":1, "nonsquarefree":1}
p=19: hist {"4":4, "13":4, "22":5, "112":3, "1111":1}
p=31: hist {"4":6, "13":11, "22":6, "112":5, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":2}
p=59: hist {"4":17, "13":19, "22":6, "112":14, "1111":1, "nonsquarefree":1}
```

Therefore Subcase A is no longer merely a proposed scan: it has finite-place
geometric `S_4` evidence at multiple primes, including `p=7`.

## What Is Not Banked

Do not promote Cycle 40 to any global theorem. The following remain unproved:

- the characteristic-zero determinant `Delta(z_0,z_1)` for either fixed
  subcase cover;
- the numerator of `disc_X L_tau` over `Q(i)(z_0,z_1)`;
- a good-reduction prime certified from the characteristic-zero branch data;
- the tame-specialization bridge from a finite-place `S_4` certificate to the
  characteristic-zero geometric group;
- the Chebotarev/Lang-Weil density step for a growing-prime set;
- any `COUNTERPACKET`, corrected-reserve theorem, MCA/CA/list/line/curve-MCA
  claim, protocol claim, SNARK claim, or prize-level solution.

## Exact New Wall

The live wall is sharpened to:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-CHAR0DELTA
```

The next theorem-sized target is to compute or certify, separately for
`ell=alpha` and `ell=-2X`, the characteristic-zero branch determinant and
quartic discriminant data needed to prove good reduction at at least one of
the finite primes already carrying geometric `S_4` evidence.

1	# Cycle 39 Symbolic Good-Reduction Audit
2	
3	Status: PROOF / BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT.
4	
5	Harness status: clean `artifact_stream` run. The mathematical answer is
6	preserved in `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RESPONSE.md`; raw
7	provider receipts are preserved separately and terminal/ad text is not involved.
8	
9	Source artifacts:
10	
11	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RESPONSE.md`
12	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RAW.json`
13	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RAW.jsonl`
14	- `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RUN_RESULT.json`
15	- `local_checks/20260618_cycle39_locator_collapse_verify.py`
16	- `local_checks/20260618_cycle39_locator_collapse_verify_result.json`
17	
18	## Ledger
19	
20	- `q_gen = p`.
21	- `B = F_p`.
22	- `F = F_{p^2} = B(alpha)`, with `alpha^2 = -1`.
23	- `q_line = |F| = p^2`.
24	- `q_chal`: unused.
25	- Domain: `D = F_p`, so `n = p`.
26	- Restricted regime: `t = sigma = 2`, `j = n-a = r-t = 4`.
27	- Candidate family: primes `p = 3 mod 4`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`,
28	  `u=[W]_E=1+X`, and top free data
29	  `W_{n-1..n-4}=1, alpha, 1+alpha, 1`.
30	
31	This remains a restricted residue-line / surface-monodromy branch. It is not
32	a corrected-reserve theorem, MCA claim, list-decoding claim, CA claim,
33	line-decoding claim, curve-MCA claim, protocol claim, SNARK claim, prize
34	solution, or `COUNTERPACKET`.
35	
36	## Banked Locator-Collapse Lemma
37	
38	Cycle 39 proves the following restricted lemma.
39	
40	Let `p = 3 mod 4`, `p != 5`, `B=F_p`,
41	`F=F_{p^2}=B(alpha)` with `alpha^2=-1`, and
42	`E=X^2+alpha X+1`. In `A=F[X]/(E)`, put `b=[X]_E` and
43	
44	```text
45	ell = [X^p - X]_E.
46	```
47	
48	Then:
49	
50	```text
51	ell = alpha*1_A    if (-5/p)=+1,
52	ell = -2*b         if (-5/p)=-1.
53	```
54	
55	Equivalently, since `p=3 mod 4`:
56	
57	```text
58	Subcase A: p = 2,3 mod 5, ell = alpha.
59	Subcase B: p = 1,4 mod 5, ell = -2X.
60	```
61	
62	Proof sketch, audited by Codex:
63	
64	1. `disc(E)=alpha^2-4=-5`. For `p != 5`, `E` is separable.
65	2. Every element of `F_p^*` is a square in `F_{p^2}^*`, so `E` splits in
66	   `F` and `A ~= F x F`.
67	3. At a root `rho`, `ell(rho)=rho^p-rho`.
68	4. Frobenius fixes `B=F_p` and sends `alpha` to `-alpha` because
69	   `p=3 mod 4`.
70	5. If `sqrt(-5) in F_p`, both roots have imaginary part `-1/2`, so
71	   `rho^p-rho=alpha`.
72	6. If `sqrt(-5) notin F_p`, each root is purely `alpha`-imaginary, so
73	   `rho^p-rho=-2rho`, hence `ell=-2b`.
74	
75	Codex added a finite sanity checker, not as proof but as a regression guard,
76	and verified the formula on sample primes from both subcases:
77	
78	```text
79	python3 experimental/2026-06-18-fable-loop/local_checks/20260618_cycle39_locator_collapse_verify.py
80	```
81	
82	The output has `all_ok=true`.
83	
84	## Route Consequence
85	
86	Before Cycle 39, the obstruction was that `[X^p-X]_E` seemed to make the
87	quartic surface family genuinely `p`-dependent, so a finite `p=31` certificate
88	could not obviously globalize.
89	
90	Cycle 39 removes that obstruction. Inside each subcase, `ell` is fixed:
91	
92	- Subcase A uses `ell=alpha`.
93	- Subcase B uses `ell=-2X`.
94	
95	Thus the equations
96	
97	```text
98	G(z,tau)=(u-z*b)*lambda(tau)-ell*[Q_S(tau)]_E=0
99	```
100	
101	define a fixed two-dimensional `B`-surface model per subcase. The remaining
102	good-reduction/monodromy question is no longer moving with `p`.
103	
104	Cycle 39 also correctly observes that the existing `p=31` certificate lies
105	only in Subcase B, since `31 = 1 mod 5` and hence `(-5/31)=-1`. It gives no
106	direct certificate for Subcase A.
107	
108	## Exact New Wall
109	
110	The live wall is:
111	
112	```text
113	W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-SUBCASE
114	```
115	
116	The next theorem-sized target is to prove, separately for `ell=alpha` and
117	`ell=-2X`, at least one of:
118	
119	1. a symbolic characteristic-zero certificate over `Q(i)(z0,z1)`:
120	   geometric irreducibility of the quartic, irreducibility of the cubic
121	   resolvent, and nonconstant squarefree discriminant part;
122	2. an explicit good-reduction certificate at one small prime per subcase,
123	   proving that the branch divisor remains etale, `Delta` keeps its degree,
124	   and the quartic cover remains separable;
125	3. a finite-place `S_4` certificate for Subcase A, analogous to the Cycle 38
126	   `p=31` Subcase B certificate;
127	4. a `ROUTE_CUT` showing that one subcase is reducible, trapped in `A_4`, or
128	   otherwise cannot yield the intended restricted seed.
129	
130	If the good-reduction bridge is proved in each subcase, the finite-place
131	monodromy certificate can potentially globalize to all but finitely many good
132	primes in that subcase. That still would be only a restricted local
133	counterpacket seed until the source-to-prize implications are separately
134	proved.
135	
136	## What Is Not Banked
137	
138	Do not bank:
139	
140	- a full solution or disproof of the Proximity Prize;
141	- a proof of corrected-reserve RS-MCA;
142	- a `COUNTERPACKET`;
143	- a uniform growing-prime `Theta(q_line)` theorem;
144	- Subcase A monodromy;
145	- good reduction at `p=31`;
146	- geometric `S_4` over `Q(i)(z0,z1)` for either subcase;
147	- an MCA, CA, list-decoding, line-decoding, curve-MCA, protocol, or SNARK
148	  statement;
149	- any merge of `q_gen=p` with `q_line=p^2`.
150	
151	## Cycle 40 Direction
152	
153	Ask the next worker to attack:
154	
155	```text
156	W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-SUBCASE
157	```
158	
159	The highest-value route is a subcase-separated certificate:
160	
161	- prove/compute Subcase A (`ell=alpha`) at `p=7`, `23`, `43`, or `47`;
162	- prove/compute Subcase B (`ell=-2X`) at `p=11`, `19`, `31`, or `59`;
163	- write symbolic `Delta`/discriminant/resolvent data over `Q(i)(z0,z1)` if
164	  feasible;
165	- otherwise identify the exact obstruction below this wall.
166

- `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RAW.json`
- `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RAW.jsonl`
- `raw/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_RUN_RESULT.json`
- `local_checks/20260618_cycle39_locator_collapse_verify.py`
- `local_checks/20260618_cycle39_locator_collapse_verify_result.json`

## Ledger

- `q_gen = p`.
- `B = F_p`.
- `F = F_{p^2} = B(alpha)`, with `alpha^2 = -1`.
- `q_line = |F| = p^2`.
- `q_chal`: unused.
- Domain: `D = F_p`, so `n = p`.
- Restricted regime: `t = sigma = 2`, `j = n-a = r-t = 4`.
- Candidate family: primes `p = 3 mod 4`, `E=X^2+alpha X+1`, `b=X`,
  `u=[W]_E=1+X`, and top free data
  `W_{n-1..n-4}=1, alpha, 1+alpha, 1`.

This remains a restricted residue-line / surface-monodromy branch. It is not
a corrected-reserve theorem, MCA claim, list-decoding claim, CA claim,
line-decoding claim, curve-MCA claim, protocol claim, SNARK claim, prize
solution, or `COUNTERPACKET`.

## Banked Locator-Collapse Lemma

Cycle 39 proves the following restricted lemma.

Let `p = 3 mod 4`, `p != 5`, `B=F_p`,
`F=F_{p^2}=B(alpha)` with `alpha^2=-1`, and
`E=X^2+alpha X+1`. In `A=F[X]/(E)`, put `b=[X]_E` and

```text
ell = [X^p - X]_E.
```

Then:

```text
ell = alpha*1_A    if (-5/p)=+1,
ell = -2*b         if (-5/p)=-1.
```

Equivalently, since `p=3 mod 4`:

```text
Subcase A: p = 2,3 mod 5, ell = alpha.
Subcase B: p = 1,4 mod 5, ell = -2X.
```

Proof sketch, audited by Codex:

1. `disc(E)=alpha^2-4=-5`. For `p != 5`, `E` is separable.
2. Every element of `F_p^*` is a square in `F_{p^2}^*`, so `E` splits in
   `F` and `A ~= F x F`.
3. At a root `rho`, `ell(rho)=rho^p-rho`.
4. Frobenius fixes `B=F_p` and sends `alpha` to `-alpha` because
   `p=3 mod 4`.
5. If `sqrt(-5) in F_p`, both roots have imaginary part `-1/2`, so
   `rho^p-rho=alpha`.
6. If `sqrt(-5) notin F_p`, each root is purely `alpha`-imaginary, so
   `rho^p-rho=-2rho`, hence `ell=-2b`.

Codex added a finite sanity checker, not as proof but as a regression guard,
and verified the formula on sample primes from both subcases:

```text
python3 experimental/2026-06-18-fable-loop/local_checks/20260618_cycle39_locator_collapse_verify.py
```

The output has `all_ok=true`.

## Route Consequence

Before Cycle 39, the obstruction was that `[X^p-X]_E` seemed to make the
quartic surface family genuinely `p`-dependent, so a finite `p=31` certificate
could not obviously globalize.

Cycle 39 removes that obstruction. Inside each subcase, `ell` is fixed:

- Subcase A uses `ell=alpha`.
- Subcase B uses `ell=-2X`.

Thus the equations

```text
G(z,tau)=(u-z*b)*lambda(tau)-ell*[Q_S(tau)]_E=0
```

define a fixed two-dimensional `B`-surface model per subcase. The remaining
good-reduction/monodromy question is no longer moving with `p`.

Cycle 39 also correctly observes that the existing `p=31` certificate lies
only in Subcase B, since `31 = 1 mod 5` and hence `(-5/31)=-1`. It gives no
direct certificate for Subcase A.

## Exact New Wall

The live wall is:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-SUBCASE
```

The next theorem-sized target is to prove, separately for `ell=alpha` and
`ell=-2X`, at least one of:

1. a symbolic characteristic-zero certificate over `Q(i)(z0,z1)`:
   geometric irreducibility of the quartic, irreducibility of the cubic
   resolvent, and nonconstant squarefree discriminant part;
2. an explicit good-reduction certificate at one small prime per subcase,
   proving that the branch divisor remains etale, `Delta` keeps its degree,
   and the quartic cover remains separable;
3. a finite-place `S_4` certificate for Subcase A, analogous to the Cycle 38
   `p=31` Subcase B certificate;
4. a `ROUTE_CUT` showing that one subcase is reducible, trapped in `A_4`, or
   otherwise cannot yield the intended restricted seed.

If the good-reduction bridge is proved in each subcase, the finite-place
monodromy certificate can potentially globalize to all but finitely many good
primes in that subcase. That still would be only a restricted local
counterpacket seed until the source-to-prize implications are separately
proved.

## What Is Not Banked

Do not bank:

- a full solution or disproof of the Proximity Prize;
- a proof of corrected-reserve RS-MCA;
- a `COUNTERPACKET`;
- a uniform growing-prime `Theta(q_line)` theorem;
- Subcase A monodromy;
- good reduction at `p=31`;
- geometric `S_4` over `Q(i)(z0,z1)` for either subcase;
- an MCA, CA, list-decoding, line-decoding, curve-MCA, protocol, or SNARK
  statement;
- any merge of `q_gen=p` with `q_line=p^2`.

## Cycle 40 Direction

Ask the next worker to attack:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-SUBCASE
```

The highest-value route is a subcase-separated certificate:

- prove/compute Subcase A (`ell=alpha`) at `p=7`, `23`, `43`, or `47`;
- prove/compute Subcase B (`ell=-2X`) at `p=11`, `19`, `31`, or `59`;
- write symbolic `Delta`/discriminant/resolvent data over `Q(i)(z0,z1)` if
  feasible;
- otherwise identify the exact obstruction below this wall.

721	kappa J_A      = -(ell wedge b)m + P_E(b,ell)w_1,
722	kappa J_Aprime = -(u wedge ell)m - P_E(u,ell)w_1,
723	```
724	
725	and their resultant is the Cycle 20 gate
726	
727	```text
728	D=(ell wedge b)P_E(u,ell)+P_E(b,ell)(u wedge ell).
729	```
730	
731	The live wall is now the `D=0` compatibility condition. Decide whether
732	
733	```text
734	Delta1==0,
735	D==0,
736	Im_alpha(p1+q2)=0,
737	Im_alpha(det P)=0
738	```
739	
740	force the leading-data ratio
741	
742	```text
743	(W_{n-2}+cW_{n-1}:W_{n-1})
744	```
745	
746	onto the kernel line of
747	
748	```text
749	[(ell wedge b), -P_E(b,ell);
750	 (u wedge ell),  P_E(u,ell)].
751	```
752	
753	If yes, the `D=0` branch collapses by the banked gates. If no, an off-kernel
754	source-valid family is the first serious seed for a `Theta(q_line)`
755	counterpacket in this sub-reserve toy window.
756	
757	This remains sub-reserve (`eta_reserve=2/n`) and must not be promoted to a
758	corrected-reserve, `q_gen`, protocol, list-decoding, line-decoding, CA, MCA, or
759	SNARK statement.
760	
761	## W-F1-AA-RES-T2J3-B-RANKONE-DESCENT
762	
763	Status: EXACT_NEW_WALL / superseded by `W-F1-AA-RES-T2J3-D-KERNEL-ALIGNMENT`.
764	
765	Cycle 20 banks the rank-one/gate structure in the restricted
766	`D=F_p`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, off-`R0` window:
767	
768	```text
769	q1=c_b eta,
770	eta=(c^2-d)+c tau_1+tau_2,
771	w^+-=(+-sqrt(delta_z)-A/kappa)/(2c_b eta)
772	```
773	
774	on the `Delta1==0` quadric branch, where `delta_z in B[tau_1,tau_2]`.
775	
776	The `P^2(F)` coefficient-image gate is
777	
778	```text
779	D=(ell wedge b)P_E(u,ell)+P_E(b,ell)(u wedge ell),
780	det M=(c_b/kappa^2)D.
781	```
782	
783	The open wall is finer than `D=0`: decide whether the base-descent equations
784	
785	```text
786	Im_alpha(p1+q2)=0,
787	Im_alpha(det P)=0
788	```
789	
790	force
791	
792	```text
793	dw wedge d eta == 0,
794	```
795	
796	hence `dim_B Im(w)=1` and `C2=O(p)`, or whether there is source-valid data with
797	`dw wedge d eta != 0`, giving a seed for `C2=Theta(p^2)=Theta(q_line)` over
798	growing primes.
799	
800	This remains sub-reserve (`eta_reserve=2/n`) and must not be promoted to a
801	corrected-reserve, `q_gen`, protocol, list-decoding, line-decoding, CA, MCA, or
802	SNARK statement.

804	## W-F1-AA-RES-T2J3-RESONANCE-SLOPE-MAP-COLLAPSE
805	
806	Status: EXACT_NEW_WALL.
807	
808	Cycle 18 banks a restricted monicity lemma in the `D=F_p`,
809	`t=sigma=2`, `j=3`, off-`R0` window. In the `{[W]_E,b}` basis,
810	
811	```text
812	Delta(tau)=wedge_{W,b}(iota,mu)
813	          =(p1-tau_3)(q2-tau_3)-p2 q1
814	```
815	
816	is monic quadratic in `tau_3`. Splitting `Delta=Delta0+alpha Delta1` over
817	`F=B+alpha B` gives `Delta0` monic degree `2` in `tau_3` and
818	`deg_{tau_3} Delta1<=1`.
819	
820	The safe side remains: if `Delta0,Delta1` have no common component over the
821	algebraic closure of `B`, then `C2=O(p)`.
822	
823	The active wall is the non-coprime resonance branch. Since
824	`deg_{tau_3} Delta1<=1`, the branch is either `Delta1==0` or a graph
825	`tau_3=-h/s`. In the graph case, the slope map reduces to
826	
827	```text
828	z(tau_1,tau_2)=(p1+h/s)/q1,
829	```
830	
831	equivalently to the coefficient map
832	
833	```text
834	mu_coef : (tau_1,tau_2) -> [q1 : (p1-q2) : p2] in P^2(F).
835	```
836	
837	The next task is to prove this rational map has one-dimensional image on every
838	source-valid non-coprime stratum, forcing `C2=O(p)`, or construct a
839	growing-prime source-valid family with `C2=Theta(p^2)=Theta(q_line)`.
840	
841	Do not promote this sub-reserve toy-window wall to a corrected-reserve theorem,
842	`q_gen` statement, protocol claim, list-decoding statement, CA, MCA, or
843	line-decoding statement.
844	
845	## W-F1-AA-RES-T2J3: Next Finite Extension Bad-Line Incidence Wall
846	
847	Status: EXACT_NEW_WALL.
848	
849	Cycle 11 closes the first finite bad-line incidence regime `t=sigma=2`,
850	`j=n-a=r-t=2`: in that regime
851	
852	```text
853	Q_S = C(X-s_T)+C1
854	```
855	
856	depends only on the co-support sum, and bad-line landing is one conic
857	`det(s_T,p_T)=0`, giving `C2=O(n)` under the stated nonresonance hypotheses.
858	
859	The next target is:
860	
861	```text
862	W-F1-AA-RES-T2J3:
863	prove or refute the bad-line incidence law for t=2, j=3.
864	```
865	
866	Reason: at `j=3`, `deg Q_S<=2`, so the quotient should acquire dependence on
867	both elementary co-support parameters and the special Cycle 11 sum-only
868	rigidity is expected to fail. The task is to determine whether a replacement
869	low-degree incidence invariant still bounds slopes, or whether a finite
870	counterpacket appears.
871	
872	Secondary target:
873	
874	```text
875	W-F1-AA-RES-T3J2:
876	analyze t=3, j=2, where the bad line has codimension two in F[X]/E.
877	```
878	
879	Do not treat the solved `t=2,j=2` calculation as evidence for the full
880	corrected-reserve conjecture.
881	
882	## W-F1-AA-RES-LINE-INCIDENCE: Reserve-Indexed Extension Bad-Line Incidence
883	
884	Status: EXACT_NEW_WALL.
885	
886	Question:
887	
888	After Cycles 1-5, in quadratic extensions with `D subset B`, arbitrary anchors reduce to the paired base interpolation-residue readout
889	
890	```text
891	S -> (interp_S(w0) mod Ehat, interp_S(w1) mod Ehat).
892	```
893	
894	Cycle 4 verifies the source ledger:
895	
896	```text
897	a=ceil((1-delta)n), sigma=a-k, balanced t=sigma, hence k+t=a=s_delta.
898	```
899	
900	Thus high agreement is automatic on the `a`-subset in the balanced regime. Can one prove a slope-image/bad-locus packing theorem for this paired readout, or produce a finite balanced arbitrary-anchor counterpacket?
901	
902	Cycle 5 sharpens the live wall:
903	
904	- Restored `W-F1-AA` is a faithful arbitrary-anchor extension-denominator instance of `Lambda^{NC}_{t,delta}` / `prob:perfiber`.
905	- Tangent/zero-numerator and quotient-periodic separation are necessary but non-binding.
906	- The banked `sigma=1` fixed-rate counterpacket survives both and yields `Theta(q_line)` slopes.
907	- Therefore the missing axis is reserve `eta=sigma/n`, and the first missing invariant is a rigidity/value-count law for the paired readout on the bad line, analogous to `thm:rigidcyclo` / `thm:exactcount` for the monic prefix object.
908	
909	Cycle 6B sharpens this again:
910	
911	- The same-slope kernel is not the wall. On `a=k+t` supports,
912	  equal slopes only says `interp_S(w)-interp_S'(w) in E*F_{<k}[X]`.
913	- The paired base readout already determines `[interp_S(w)]_E`.
914	- These facts describe the slope map's kernel/descent, not the number of
915	  distinct slope values.
916	- The actual wall is a value-count / collision law for the image of the paired
917	  readout on the bad line `F*[Bnum]_E`.
918	
919	Cycle 7 cuts a tempting but false transfer:
920	
921	- A clean structured Claude answer claimed a `BANKABLE_LEMMA` by transferring
922	  the `z in B` stratum to a base residue-line datum
923	  `(Ehat,b_hat,w0+theta*w1)`.
924	- Source audit rejects that transfer. The CRT class
925	  `theta in B[X]/Ehat` is generally nonconstant, and multiplication by
926	  `theta` does not commute with support interpolation:
927	  `theta * interp_S(w1)` is not generally `interp_S(theta*w1)` modulo `Ehat`.
928	- Therefore `thm:exactcount` / `thm:rigidcyclo` cannot be imported verbatim
929	  through the claimed base datum.
930	- The live object is a twisted readout:
931	
932	```text
933	S -> [interp_S(w0)]_Ehat + theta [interp_S(w1)]_Ehat.
934	```
935	
936	Cycle 8 resolves the twist:
937	
938	- In the separated case `gcd(E,E^tau)=1`, reduction modulo `E` gives a ring
939	  isomorphism `B[X]/Ehat ~= F[X]/E`.
940	- Under this isomorphism, the twisted readout is exactly the original extension
941	  residue `[interp_S(w0)+alpha interp_S(w1)]_E`.
942	- The non-absorption commutator is real but locator-divisible:
943	  `theta interp_S(w1)-interp_S(theta w1)=L_S R_S`,
944	  `deg R_S<=2t-2`.
945	- Thus the twist is inert for value counting. The hard object is the extension
946	  residue count itself.
947	
948	Cycle 9 corrects the count being asked for:
949	
950	- The raw residue set
951	  `C1=#{[interp_S(w0)+alpha interp_S(w1)]_E}` is not the source object in
952	  `def:residue` / `thm:normalform`.
953	- The source object is the slope set
954	  `C2=#{z in F : exists S, [interp_S(w)]_E = z[Bnum]_E}`.
955	- Equivalently, it is a landing/incidence count into the bad line
956	  `F[Bnum]_E subset F[X]/E`.
957	- Since `dim_F F[X]/E=t` and `dim_F F[Bnum]_E=1` for nonzero numerator, the
958	  reserve mechanism is a codimension-`(t-1)` incidence problem. The
959	  `sigma=1` counterpacket is exactly the codimension-zero endpoint where raw
960	  residues and slopes coincide.
961	- The locator-quotient identity
962	  `W=L_S Q_S+interp_S(w)`, `deg Q_S<=n-a-1`, gives
963	  `[interp_S(w)]_E=[W]_E-[L_S Q_S]_E`.
964	
965	Current target:
966	
967	```text
968	bound the number of slopes z in F for which some a-subset S satisfies
969	[interp_S(w0)+alpha interp_S(w1)]_E = z[Bnum]_E.
970	```
971	
972	Equivalently:
973	
974	```text
975	[L_S Q_S]_E lands in [W]_E - F[Bnum]_E,
976	where W=interp_D(w), W=L_S Q_S+interp_S(w), deg Q_S<=j-1, j=n-a=r-t.
977	```
978	
979	Parameters to keep separate:
980	
981	- `B=F_p`, generated/entropy field, `q_gen=p`.
982	- `F=F_{p^2}`, line/extension field, `q_line=p^2`.
983	- `q_chal` is protocol challenge field only after a protocol theorem.
984	- `sigma=t=2` is the first finite balanced test case.
985	
986	Success outputs:
987	
988	- BANKABLE_LEMMA: prove a source-valid incidence/collision lemma for landings
989	  of `[L_S Q_S]_E` in the affine bad line `[W]_E-F[Bnum]_E`, even in a
990	  restricted but meaningful regime.
991	- COUNTERPACKET: explicit finite or symbolic data showing that the reserve-indexed wall is false above corrected reserve, with field ledgers separated.
992	- ROUTE_CUT: show `W-F1-AA-RES-LINE-INCIDENCE` is not source-valid and identify
993	  the false reduction.
994	- EXACT_NEW_WALL: identify the first missing invariant sharper than the current
995	  bad-line incidence law.
996	
997	Non-goals:
998	
999	- Do not attempt a raw uniform fiber bound over all arbitrary `w0,w1`; low-degree anchors make that statement false.
1000	- Do not reopen the balanced nonzero-numerator support-shrinking noncontainment issue; it is handled by the Cycle 3 lemma.
1001	- Do not reintroduce `W-F1-AA-AGR` as a balanced wall; Cycle 4 cuts it by `a=s_delta`.
1002	- Do not ask another worker to prove only the same-slope kernel
1003	  `E*F_{<k}[X]`; Cycle 6B shows that is a tautological restatement, not a
1004	  value-count theorem.
1005	- Do not absorb the nonconstant CRT multiplier `theta` into the base word as
1006	  `w0+theta*w1` without a theorem; Cycle 7 gives a concrete local check showing
1007	  this commutation fails in general.
1008	- Do not reopen `W-F1-AA-RES-TWISTED-READOUT` as the live wall; Cycle 8 shows
1009	  the twist is isomorphic to the original extension residue count.
1010	- Do not treat the raw residue value count `C1` as the MCA slope count; Cycle 9
1011	  shows the source-relevant count is the bad-line incidence/slope count `C2`.
1012	- Do not use the sub-reserve `sigma=1` counterpacket as a corrected-reserve refutation.
1013	- Do not claim `ass:extension-mca-lift`, a protocol denominator saving, or a line-decoding theorem from the paired-readout reduction alone.
1014	
1015	Cycle 11 status update:
1016	
1017	- The restricted case `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=2` is now BANKABLE_LEMMA / AUDIT.
1018	- The general line-incidence wall remains open.
1019	- The immediate next finite wall is `t=2,j=3`.
1020	
1021	Cycle 12 status update:

1023	- The `t=sigma=2`, `j=n-a=3` quotient algebra is now BANKABLE_LEMMA / AUDIT.

1036	
1037	Concrete question:

1040	the elementary-symmetric `(e1,e2,e3)` space. How many `D`-split cubics
1041

1045	
1046	can lie on `ell_z`? If the average fiber is `Theta(n)`, then `C2=Theta(n)`;

1062	Delta_i in B[e1,e2,e3].

1064	
1065	- Therefore the generic landing locus is a complete-intersection curve of

1070	Refined immediate wall:
1071

1074	```
1075	
1076	Prove or refute that `Delta_0` and `Delta_1` have no common surface component
1077	outside classified resonance strata. If true for `D=F_p`, then
1078	`C2<=#landings=O(p)=O(n)` without proving small fixed-slope fibers.

1080	Cycle 13 status update:
1081	
1082	- The base-component complete-intersection route is now BANKABLE_LEMMA / AUDIT
1083	  off the resonance strata.
1084	- Banked identity:
1085

1088	```
1089	
1090	with `Res(L_T,E) != 0` on valid co-supports.

1092	- Off

1101	`C2=O(n)` for `D=F_p`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=3`.
1102	
1103	Current exact wall:

1108	
1109	Question: on `Ra` and `Rb`, do many split cubics land with many distinct

1114	Cycle 14 status update:
1115	
1116	- `Ra/Rb` are not source-excluded by the currently banked hypotheses.
1117	- On `Ra/Rb`, the landing locus can be a base surface, so Cycle 13's
1118	  complete-intersection `#landings=O(p)` argument does not apply.
1119	- The remaining obstruction is the slope map on that surface.
1120	
1121	Current exact wall:
1122	
1123	```text
1124	W-F1-AA-RES-T2J3-RANK-DET-SPLIT.
1125	```
1126	
1127	Cycle 15 sharpens the Cycle 14 surface slope-fiber problem. On a resonance
1128	surface `Sigma subset B^3`, with
1129	
1130	```text
1131	iota=A0(tau_1,tau_2)-tau_3[W]_E,
1132	mu=B0(tau_1,tau_2)-tau_3 b,

1137	Writing the `B`-linear part of `L_z` as columns

1142	
1143	the consistency determinant is

1148	
1149	Rank alone is not the invariant. For fixed `z`, rank `3` gives an affine
1150	consistency determinant; if `Q!=0`, the slope set is curve-sized `O(p)`, while
1151	rank `3` with `Q==0` identically is the possible `Theta(q_line)` slope regime.
1152	
1153	The immediate target is:
1154	
1155	- prove `Q!=0` or equivalent curve-sized image control on every source-valid
1156	  `Ra/Rb` resonance stratum; or

# Active Walls

## W-F1-AA-RES-STRUCTURED-COSUPPORT-RESERVE-LIFT

Status: EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL.

Cycle 42 external 5.5 Pro round cuts the prior A-side good-reduction
obstruction for the fixed restricted `t=2,j=4` branch. Four external answers
and returned checker/certificate artifacts agree that Cycle 41's raw
affine/Cramer `(G1,G2,G3)` gate was overstrong: its A-side failure is a false
negative caused by unsaturated/projective/common-factor behavior, not a
source-valid obstruction. Conservatively banked result:

```text
restricted fixed branch:
q_gen = p,
B = F_p,
F = F_{p^2},
q_line = p^2,
D = F_p,
t = sigma = 2,
j = 4,
N_split(p) = p^2/24 + O(p^(3/2))
           = q_line/24 + O(q_line^(3/4)).
```

This is a restricted local closure only. It is not a corrected-reserve,
fixed-rate, generated-field, MCA/list/line-decoding/curve-MCA, protocol,
SNARK, prize, or final `COUNTERPACKET` result.
